# Runtime dock binding ‚Äî station-owned device identity

> Design + module audit for moving the **dock name** off the build (per-device
> `local.properties` / `secrets.h`) and onto a station-owned `deviceId ‚Üí dock`
> binding, so devices are **named on the device side at runtime** and the name
> **survives app uninstall / firmware reflash** with zero re-entry.

## Context ‚Äî the problem

Today which dock a device belongs to is **compiled in**:

| Device | Source of dock name | Path on the wire |
|---|---|---|
| Phone (Android app) | `DOCK_NAME` in `local.properties` ‚Üí `BuildConfig.DOCK_NAME` | `StationLink.runSession()` puts it in the `hello.dock` field |
| Body (ESP32 firmware) | `#define DOCK_NAME` in `secrets.h` | `send_hello()` (`src/station_link.c`) |

Consequences: **every phone and every board needs its own build** just to be
named, and the name is brittle ‚Äî there's no way to rename a dock or move a part
to a different dock without a rebuild+reflash.

**Goal (user-confirmed):** set the dock identity from the **device side at
runtime**, and have it **persist across app uninstall and firmware reflash**.

**The hard constraint that shapes everything:** Android app-private storage
(SharedPreferences / DataStore) is *always wiped on uninstall*. So the name
cannot live only on the device. The fix is to put the **source of truth on the
station**, keyed by an identifier the device *can't* lose.

## The model ‚Äî a dock is a named composition; parts are interchangeable

This is already how the station thinks (`modules/docks/directory.ts`): a **dock**
is a *named composition of components* (its **manifest**), and the station routes
by **capability**, not by hardware ‚Äî
`resolveCap(dock, 'face')`, never "phone #5". A phone serves `voice/face/camera`
(the `phone` slot); a body serves `servo` (the `body` slot). **Parts can come from
anywhere** ‚Äî any Android phone claimed into `anne-bot` *becomes* its phone slot;
a future component slots in by the capabilities it advertises, with no station
change.

Therefore the binding only needs the **dock name**. The **slot/component is
derived from the device's `kind`** (`dock-android-app` ‚Üí `phone`,
`dock-body-fw` ‚Üí `body`), so the operator's whole job is one field:
**"this device ‚Üí which dock."**

## Stable device id ‚Äî the binding key

The key must survive uninstall/reflash. Each device already has (or can cheaply
get) one:

| Device | Stable id | Survives | Caveat |
|---|---|---|---|
| Body (ESP32) | factory **MAC** (eFuse, `esp_read_mac`) | reflash, OTA, NVS-erase | already the basis of its `id`; widen from last-3-bytes to **full 6-byte MAC** for collision-safety |
| Phone | `Settings.Secure.ANDROID_ID` | uninstall/reinstall **on the same signing key** | factory-reset or signing-key change ‚Üí new id (the unclaimed-reclaim flow is the safety net) |

**Verified for the actual fleet:** the app's `minSdk = 26` (Android 8.0), so every
supported device is in the modern ANDROID_ID regime (per app-signing-key + device,
stable across reinstall). The connected test device (Xiaomi, Android 13) returns a
valid id. **Reproducibility note:** a debug APK signed with an auto-generated debug
keystore is stable on *one* build machine but differs across machines (different
key ‚Üí different id). If the dock APK is ever built from more than one machine, pin
a shared keystore so the id stays constant.

The current phone hello `id` is an install-UUID that *is* wiped on uninstall ‚Äî
useless as the binding key. Switch the hello `id` to **ANDROID_ID**.

## Design overview

```
device boots
  ‚îî‚îÄ has cached dock name? ‚îÄ‚îÄyes‚îÄ‚îÄ‚ñ∂ hello { id, dock }            (instant re-announce)
                            ‚îî‚îÄno‚îÄ‚îÄ‚ñ∂ hello { id }  (dock omitted)  (unclaimed)

station on hello:
  if hello.dock present       ‚Üí use it (and record binding[id]=dock)
  else lookup binding[id]
        ‚îú‚îÄ found  ‚Üí set peer.dock; welcome { id, dock, component }
        ‚îî‚îÄ none   ‚Üí peer.dock stays null; welcome { id, dock:null }   (UNCLAIMED)

console: operator sees unclaimed device ‚Üí enters dock name ‚Üí POST /api/docks/bind
station: binding[id]=dock; MUTATE the live peer.dock; announce peer-updated;
         push welcome/binding frame to the peer ‚Üí it adopts + caches ‚Üí goes live
```

**Source of truth = the binding table.** The device *caches* the last dock name it
learned so it can re-announce instantly on reconnect, but the station's binding
**overrides** on conflict.

### Decisions (user-confirmed)

| Decision | Choice | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Persistence | Must survive uninstall/reflash | the core requirement |
| Assignment | **Claim unbound device from the web console** (REST underneath) | most discoverable; no cable/adb |
| Unbound window | Connect, **idle, OTA+config only**; brain/body/perception/media dormant | safe; those two already route by peer-id |
| Lifecycle state | **Implicit** ‚Äî unclaimed ‚â° `peer.dock == null` | modules already early-return on null dock; no new state machine |
| Rename data | **Leave old state under old name** as history; device starts fresh under new name | historically honest; avoids cross-module rekeying |
| Slot/component | **Derived from device `kind`**, not stored in the binding | "parts from anywhere" falls out for free |

## Module audit ‚Äî what changes, what doesn't

The central finding: **two routing styles already exist in the codebase**, and they
behave very differently for a dock-less peer.

- **Routed by peer-id** (`to: peerId`) ‚Üí **already works unclaimed, no change.**
- **Routed by `(dock, component)`** (`toAddr`, `resolveCap`) ‚Üí **silently no-ops
  while dock is null, then works the moment `peer.dock` is set and `peer-updated`
  is announced.** Crucially these modules *already* subscribe to `peer-updated` /
  re-resolve the live roster, so claiming needs **no change inside them** ‚Äî only
  the hub mutation + re-announce.

| Module | Keys on | Unclaimed (dock null) | On claim / rebind | Change needed |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **hub** (`core/hub.ts`) | ‚Äî | sends `welcome`; collision loop already guarded by `if (peer.dock && peer.component)`; `toAddr` fan-out won't match a null-dock peer | needs a **claim API**: mutate `peer.dock`, re-announce `peer-updated`, push binding frame | **YES ‚Äî the core change** |
| **ota** (`modules/ota`) | `peer.kind` + `to: peerId` | ‚úÖ works ‚Äî offers by software kind, directed by peer id | no-op | **none** |
| **config** (`modules/config`) | interest map + `to: peerId` | ‚úÖ works ‚Äî pushes by peer id; keys are flat/global (not per-dock) | no-op | **none** |
| **docks directory** (`modules/docks`) | `(dock, component)`; `noteSeen` early-returns on null dock | invisible (by design ‚Äî same path as ephemeral peers) | `noteSeen` runs on `peer-updated` ‚Üí dock appears in directory | small: **`GET /unclaimed`** + **claim/bind REST** (see below) |
| **brain** (`modules/brain`) | `session(dock)`, `dockOf(peerId)`; sessions persisted at `.data/brain/<dock>/` | agent frames already rejected with *"need a dock identity"*; no session created | session created lazily on first turn under the (now-set) dock | **none** (lazy-create already keyed off live `dockOf`) |
| **tasks** (`modules/brain/tasks`) | `(dock, instanceId)`; spawned with `TASK_DOCK` | none spawn while unclaimed (brain idle) | new tasks use new dock; old tasks stay under old dock (acceptable per "leave old state") | **none** |
| **rpc** (`modules/brain/rpc`) | `resolveCap(dock, cap)` + `to: reqId` | tool-calls can't resolve a null-dock target | resolves once dock set | **none** |
| **bodylink** (`modules/bodylink`) | `resolveCap(dock, 'servo'/'face')` + `toAddr`; already listens to `peer-joined/left` | body holds center (reuses the `STATION_URL`-empty idle path); no digests | re-resolves on next digest/command after `peer-updated` | **none** |
| **media** (`modules/media` SFU) | `streamId = peerId`; dock name only a display `label` | ‚úÖ stream works; label falls back to peer id | label refreshes via perception's live-roster `resolveDock` | **none** (but keep perception dormant while unclaimed) |
| **perception** (`modules/perception`) | `dockId` on snapshots/state/memory; `resolveDock(streamId)` walks live roster | **must stay dormant while unclaimed** ‚Äî else snapshots get `dockId=undefined` and split the timeline | starts cleanly once dock is set | small: **gate pipeline start on dock present** |
| **observability / cost** (`modules/observability`) | session `source` (the dock), captured at session creation and **immutable** | no sessions while brain idle ‚áí no mis-attribution | new sessions carry the new dock; old stay under old (matches "leave old state") | **none** (idle-while-unclaimed avoids the mis-attribution the audit flagged) |
| **web UI** (`web/src`) | dock-keyed views (Overview, Cost, Perception, Brain, Tasks, BodyLink) | unclaimed peers currently render as `dock = "‚Äî"` | ‚Äî | **YES ‚Äî add unclaimed/claim UI** (Overview or sidebar) |

**Why idle-while-unclaimed is the keystone:** the only modules that would
*mis-record* under a null/placeholder dock are **perception** (snapshot `dockId`)
and **observability/cost** (session `source`, immutable once set). Keeping brain,
perception and media dormant until claimed means nothing is ever written under a
provisional identity ‚Äî so there is **no rekeying problem to solve** and "leave old
state under old name" is the *only* state question, cleanly answered.

## Implementation plan

### Server (`orbit-station/server`)

1. **Binding store ‚Äî `modules/docks/bindings.ts` (new).** Follow the SQLite pattern
   of `modules/config/store.ts` (NOT the `docks.json` file). `orbitDb()` from
   `core/db.ts`; table:
   ```sql
   CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dock_bindings (
     device_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, dock TEXT NOT NULL, last_updated INTEGER
   );
   ```
   API: `lookup(deviceId)`, `bind(deviceId, dock)`, `unbind(deviceId)`, `list()`.
   (Slot is derived from `kind`, not stored.)

2. **Resolve binding on hello ‚Äî `core/hub.ts` `#onMessage` `hello` case (~L208).**
   - After populating the peer: if `f.dock` present, `bindings.bind(f.id, f.dock)`
     (self-bind from a dev-override). Else `peer.dock = bindings.lookup(f.id)?.dock`.
   - Extend the **welcome** frame to carry the resolved name:
     `{ t:'welcome', id, serverTime, dock: peer.dock ?? null, component: peer.component ?? null }`.
     Add `dock?` / `component?` to `WelcomeFrame` in `core/protocol.ts`.
   - The collision loop is already guarded by `if (peer.dock && peer.component)` ‚Äî
     unclaimed peers correctly don't displace anyone.

3. **Claim API + unclaimed list ‚Äî `modules/docks/index.ts`.** The hub `roster()`
   already includes dock-less peers.
   - `GET /api/docks/unclaimed` ‚Üí roster entries with no `dock` (id, kind, label,
     ip, build, lastSeen).
   - `POST /api/docks/bind { deviceId, dock }` ‚Üí `bindings.bind`; find the live peer;
     **mutate `peer.dock`** (and derive `component` from `kind`); `#announce('peer-updated', ‚Ä¶)`;
     push a directed **welcome/binding** frame (`to: peer.id`) so it adopts without
     reconnect; call `directory.noteSeen` so the dock appears.
   - `DELETE /api/docks/bind/:deviceId` ‚Üí `bindings.unbind` (re-park unclaimed).
   - This requires a small **hub method to mutate a peer's dock by id** + re-announce
     (the one genuinely new hub capability).

4. **Perception gate ‚Äî `modules/perception` (hub.ts `#startOn` / `main.ts` `resolveDock`).**
   Don't start processors when `resolveDock(streamId)` yields no real dock; start on
   the next track after the peer is claimed. Avoids `dockId=undefined` snapshots.

### Web (`orbit-station/web`)

5. **Unclaimed + Claim UI.** A `useUnclaimed()` hook (mirrors `lib/useDocks.ts`) hitting
   `/api/docks/unclaimed`; an "Unclaimed devices" panel (simplest in
   `modules/Overview.tsx`, or the always-visible `components/DockStatus.tsx`) with a
   **Claim** button ‚Üí dock-name field ‚Üí `POST /api/docks/bind`. Show `kind` + short id.

### Phone app (`node-dock/app`)

6. **Stable id:** send `Settings.Secure.ANDROID_ID` as hello `id` (new
   `station/DeviceId.kt` or extend `station/InstallId.kt`); wire at `DockScreen.kt`
   where `appId` is sourced. Comment the signing-key caveat.
7. **Stop baking the name:** `DOCK_NAME` becomes an optional dev override (empty
   default in `build.gradle.kts` + `local.properties.template`). In
   `StationLink.runSession()` (~L171) **omit `dock`** from hello when unknown.
8. **Learn + cache:** parse `welcome.dock`/`component`; persist to a
   `SharedPreferences` cache (e.g. `orbit_identity`); use it on next connect for
   instant re-announce. (Wiped on uninstall ‚Äî fine, the station re-supplies it.)
9. **Unclaimed UX:** brain/face idle; show "unclaimed ‚Äî claim me in the console
   (id: ‚Ä¶)" reusing the not-configured path in `RemoteBrain.kt`.

### Firmware (`node-dock/body-firmware/dock_body_v0`)

10. **Stable id:** widen MAC-derived id to **full 6-byte MAC** (`src/station_link.c`,
    `station_link_start` snprintf ~L325).
11. **Stop requiring `DOCK_NAME`:** omit `dock` from `send_hello()` (~L113) when
    unknown; `DOCK_NAME` in `secrets.h` becomes an optional dev override.
12. **Learn + persist to NVS:** parse `welcome.dock`; `nvs_open`/`nvs_set_str` a
    `dock` key (partition already exists; firmware currently only `nvs_flash_init`s).
    On boot read NVS ‚Üí send in hello for instant re-announce; station binding still
    overrides via welcome. **Survives reflash** (NVS is a separate partition, not
    erased by app OTA).
13. **Unclaimed behavior:** no dock ‚áí servos hold center (reuse the `STATION_URL`-empty
    idle path).

## Lifecycle scenarios (validated against the audit)

| Scenario | Result |
|---|---|
| Brand-new phone, never bound | hello `{id}` ‚Üí unclaimed ‚Üí idle (OTA/config only) ‚Üí console claim ‚Üí `peer-updated` ‚Üí brain/perception/body come alive, no rebuild |
| Phone **uninstall + reinstall** (same signing key) | same ANDROID_ID ‚Üí station `lookup` hits ‚Üí welcome returns dock ‚Üí live immediately, **no console action** |
| Board **reflash** (app partition) | NVS dock survives ‚Üí re-announces instantly; binding also still in station |
| **Rename** anne-bot ‚Üí living-room | new binding; live peer mutated + `peer-updated`; new sessions/traces/cost under `living-room`; **old state stays under `anne-bot`** as history |
| Swap to a **different phone** in same dock | new device id ‚Üí unclaimed ‚Üí claim into `anne-bot` ‚Üí it *is* the phone slot (slot derived from `kind`); old phone, if gone, leaves an offline slot |
| ANDROID_ID changes (factory reset / new signing key) | reappears unclaimed ‚Üí one-click re-claim; no data loss |

## Verification (end-to-end)

1. **Server unit:** `bindings.ts` round-trip (bind/lookup/unbind) like
   `config/store` tests; hub resolves `peer.dock` from a pre-seeded binding when
   hello omits dock (extend hub/brain tests). `cd orbit-station && npm test`.
2. **Unclaimed flow (manual, `npm run dev` + `npm run smoke`):**
   - Fake peer `hello {id:"test-1"}` (no dock) ‚Üí in `GET /api/docks/unclaimed`, NOT in `GET /api/docks`.
   - `POST /api/docks/bind {deviceId:"test-1", dock:"anne-bot"}` ‚Üí peer gets binding frame; now in `GET /api/docks`.
   - Reconnect `hello {id:"test-1"}` (still no dock) ‚Üí welcome returns `dock:"anne-bot"` (binding survived).
3. **Phone:** `./gradlew :app:installDebug` with empty `DOCK_NAME` ‚Üí connects unclaimed
   (hint shown, brain idle) ‚Üí claim in console ‚Üí live, no rebuild. **Uninstall +
   reinstall** ‚Üí auto-rebound (same ANDROID_ID), zero console action.
4. **Firmware:** flash with empty `DOCK_NAME` ‚Üí unclaimed ‚Üí claim ‚Üí live. Power-cycle ‚Üí
   re-announces from NVS. **Reflash app partition** ‚Üí NVS dock persists; still bound.
5. `cd node-dock/app && ./gradlew test` stays green.

## Rejected alternatives

- **In-app text field as the primary mechanism.** A name in SharedPreferences is
  wiped on uninstall ‚Üí fails the core requirement. (Kept as an *optional* dev override.)
- **Writing the name to shared external storage on the phone.** Permission-gated,
  user-deletable, no firmware analogue, asymmetric. The binding table reuses existing
  SQLite persistence and is identical for phone and board.
- **Migrating all module state on rename.** Cross-module rekeying (brain files,
  perception snapshots, immutable observability `source`) is risky for little gain;
  "leave old state under old name" is historically honest and idle-while-unclaimed
  means nothing is ever written under a provisional identity.
